verdicts.interest_take: The team-huddle-replaced-by-orbit image is the episode's sharpest visual essay on what Zava costs the room.
verdicts.writing_notes: Title/moment/idea/why all distinct; idea is the real payoff; warm and concise. Right category.

verdicts.interest_take: Genuine 'I never caught that' — the singer is Rupert's actor's real brother, plus the Judas/false-messiah layer.
verdicts.writing_notes: Double-Easter-egg framing is clear and fun; concise; Aesthetics fits.

verdicts.interest_take: A 1972 gibberish-that-sounds-like-English song scoring Zava's reign — delightful and thematically pointed.
verdicts.writing_notes: Honest that the Zava read is inference; clean and witty; good length.

verdicts.interest_take: Seeing Zlatan (plus Cantona/Lewandowski) in Zava recolors his swagger as affectionate parody.
verdicts.writing_notes: Trivia card done right; attribution honestly framed; warm payoff line.

verdicts.interest_take: Reframes Ted's hurt as a real professional-ethics boundary, not just jealousy — deepens the scene.
verdicts.writing_notes: Idea carries the weight; careful 'norm, not a ruling' framing; pairs with the panic-attack card without duplicating it.

verdicts.interest_take: The gap between his egolessness rhetoric and the self-centering whiteboard redraw is a sharp character read.
verdicts.writing_notes: Strong, but overlaps the Zava cluster; the whiteboard image is also nearly the meditation-circle image — kept because the focus here is rhetoric vs. ego.

verdicts.interest_take: The show's 'optimism isn't healing' thesis lands hard here; the breakthrough is feeling it, not a pep talk.
verdicts.writing_notes: Serious beat handled with warmth; idea is the payoff; no dialogue. Belief & hope fits.

verdicts.interest_take: Low surprise (it's in the name) but the formation-as-metaphor reading earns the card.
verdicts.writing_notes: Clear and concise; idea distinguishes the real shape from the thesis. Good anchor for the episode.

verdicts.interest_take: A quiet noticing that seeds a season-long arc — the weight is in what Trent doesn't do.
verdicts.writing_notes: Gentle, well-paced; idea names the arc without overexplaining. Relationships fits.

verdicts.interest_take: The coaches naming their Zava problem via a Sound of Music lament is a tidy comic-thematic joke.
verdicts.writing_notes: Distinct, light, warm; idea connects the song to the disruption cleanly.

verdicts.interest_take: Weakest survivor — low surprise (rivals-to-mentors is well-trodden) but a real arc-launch worth flagging.
verdicts.writing_notes: Solid writing; earns its spot mainly as the set's lone Redemption card and a 'watch this pairing' pointer.

verdicts.interest_take: Thinnest card: 'this song is on the soundtrack and its title fits the theme' — generic, mostly interpretation.
verdicts.writing_notes: Relevance/insight low; third Aesthetics/music card after two stronger ones (Superstar, Prisencolinensinainciusol) crowds the set.

episode_notes: Strong set, lightly over-stuffed in two clusters. ZAVA CLUSTER (title-formation, zava-empty-vessel, meditation-circle, zava-zlatan) is four cards on one figure; they survive because each works a different angle (tactics/thesis, rhetoric-vs-ego, staging, real-world inspiration), but empty-vessel and meditation-circle both peak on the same self-centering image — empty-vessel is the more cuttable of the two if a further trim is wanted. MUSIC/AESTHETICS CLUSTER had three soundtrack cards; cut everybody-knows-cohen (generic 'song fits theme' interpretation) leaving the two genuine winners (Superstar family in-joke, Prisencolinensinainciusol gibberish). Category variety after the cut is good: Meta & trivia x3, Aesthetics x2, Ethics & character x2, Relationships x2, Belief & hope x1, Redemption x1. The dr-jacob-ethics and ted-panic-attack cards pair well (ethics deepens the emotional beat) without duplicating. Best-first ordering leads with the three highest-surprise winners (meditation-circle, Superstar, Prisencolinensinainciusol). Two forward-scope flags (colin-comes-out-trent, roy-trains-jamie) are season-arc setups, not finale spoilers — acceptable for a S1-rewatch gift but flagged. roy-trains-jamie is the marginal keep; cut it too if a tighter set is preferred. No reproduced dialogue; the 'Maria' and 'Superstar' titles are work titles, not show lines.
